 90â†’/**
 91â†’ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 92â†’ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 93â†’ *
 94â†’ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 95â†’ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 96â†’ * indentation rule is not run.
 97â†’ */
 98â†’public class WrappingRule :
 99â†’    StandardRule(
100â†’        id = "wrapping",
101â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
102â†’            setOf(
103â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
104â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
105â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
106â†’            ),
107â†’        visitorModifiers =
108â†’            setOf(
109â†’                RunAfterRule(
110â†’                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
111â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
112â†’                ),
113â†’            ),
114â†’    ) {
115â†’    private var line = 1
116â†’    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
117â†’    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
118â†’
119â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
120â†’        line = 1
121â†’        indentConfig =
122â†’            IndentConfig(
123â†’                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
124â†’                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
125â†’            )
126â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
127â†’    }
128â†’
129â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
130â†’        node: ASTNode,
131â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
132â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
133â†’    ) {
134â†’        when (node.elementType) {
135â†’            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136â†’            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137â†’            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
138â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139â†’            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
140â†’            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
141â†’            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
142â†’            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
143â†’            SEMICOLON -> insertNewLineAfterSemi(node, autoCorrect, emit)
144â†’        }
145â†’    }
146â†’
147â†’    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
148â†’        node: ASTNode,
149â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
151â†’    ) {
152â†’        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
153â†’
154â†’        val lbrace =
155â†’            node
156â†’                .getStartOfBlock()
157â†’                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
158â†’                ?: return
159â†’        if (lbrace.followedByNewline() ||
160â†’            lbrace.followedByEolComment() ||
161â†’            lbrace.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY)
162â†’        ) {
163â†’            // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
164â†’            return
165â†’        }
166â†’
167â†’        node
168â†’            .takeUnless { it.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
169â†’            ?.getEndOfBlock()
170â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
171â†’            ?.let { rbrace ->
172â†’                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
173â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
174â†’                }
175â†’            }
176â†’
177â†’        if (maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
178â†’            val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
179â†’                node
180â†’                    .leaves(false)
181â†’                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
182â†’                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
183â†’            val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
184â†’                node
185â†’                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
186â†’                    .leavesIncludingSelf()
187â†’                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
188â†’                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
189â†’            if (lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength) {
190â†’                requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
191â†’            }
192â†’        }
193â†’    }
194â†’
195â†’    private fun ASTNode.followedByEolComment() =
196â†’        null !=
197â†’            leaves()
198â†’                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
199â†’                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
200â†’
201â†’    private fun rearrangeBlock(
202â†’        node: ASTNode,
203â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
204â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
205â†’    ) {
206â†’        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
207â†’        var newlineInBetween = false
208â†’        var parameterListInBetween = false
209â†’        var numberOfArgs = 0
210â†’        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
211â†’        // matching ), ] or }
212â†’        val r = node.nextSibling {
213â†’            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
214â†’            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
215â†’            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
216â†’            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
217â†’            if (isValueArgument) {
218â†’                numberOfArgs++
219â†’                firstArg = it
220â†’            }
221â†’            it.elementType == rElementType
222â†’        }!!
223â†’        if (
224â†’            !newlineInBetween ||
225â†’            // keep { p ->
226â†’            // }
227â†’            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
228â†’            // keep ({
229â†’            // }) and (object : C {
230â†’            // })
231â†’            (
232â†’                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
233â†’                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
234â†’                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
235â†’                )
236â†’        ) {
237â†’            return
238â†’        }
239â†’        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
240â†’            // keep:
241â†’            // for (foo in listOf(
242â†’            //     "foo-1",
243â†’            //     "foo-2"
244â†’            // )) { ... }
245â†’            // but reject:
246â†’            // for (
247â†’            //     foo in listOf(
248â†’            //         "foo-1",
249â†’            //         "foo-2"
250â†’            //     )
251â†’            // ) { ... }
252â†’            return
253â†’        }
254â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
255â†’                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
256â†’                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
257â†’                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
258â†’                    it.textLength > 0
259â†’            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
260â†’            // IDEA quirk:
261â†’            // if (true &&
262â†’            //     true
263â†’            // ) {
264â†’            // }
265â†’            // instead of
266â†’            // if (
267â†’            //     true &&
268â†’            //     true
269â†’            // ) {
270â†’            // }
271â†’            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
272â†’        ) {
273â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
274â†’        }
275â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
276â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
277â†’        }
278â†’    }
279â†’
280â†’    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
281â†’        node: ASTNode,
282â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
283â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
284â†’    ) {
285â†’        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
286â†’        if (
287â†’            node.textContains('\n') &&
288â†’            entries.size > 1 &&
289â†’            // e.g.
290â†’            //
291â†’            // class A : B, C,
292â†’            //     D
293â†’            // or
294â†’            // class A : B, C({
295â†’            // }), D
296â†’            //
297â†’            // but not
298â†’            //
299â†’            // class A : B, C, D({
300â†’            // })
301â†’            !(
302â†’                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
303â†’                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
304â†’                )
305â†’        ) {
306â†’            // put space after :
307â†’            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
308â†’                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
309â†’                if (
310â†’                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
311â†’                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
312â†’                ) {
313â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
314â†’                }
315â†’            }
316â†’            // put entries on separate lines
317â†’            for (c in node.children()) {
318â†’                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
319â†’                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
320â†’                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
321â†’                ) {
322â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
323â†’                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
324â†’                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
325â†’                        emit = emit,
326â†’                        indent = node.indent(),
327â†’                    )
328â†’                }
329â†’            }
330â†’        }
331â†’    }
332â†’
333â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
334â†’        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
335â†’            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
336â†’
337â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(
338â†’        node: ASTNode,
339â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
340â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
341â†’    ) {
342â†’        for (c in node.children()) {
343â†’            val hasLineBreak =
344â†’                when (c.elementType) {
345â†’                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
346â†’                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
347â†’                    else -> false
348â†’                }
349â†’            if (hasLineBreak) {
350â†’                // rearrange
351â†’                //
352â†’                // a, b, value(
353â†’                // ), c, d
354â†’                //
355â†’                // to
356â†’                //
357â†’                // a, b,
358â†’                // value(
359â†’                // ),
360â†’                // c, d
361â†’
362â†’                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
363â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
364â†’                if (
365â†’                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
366â†’                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
367â†’                ) {
368â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
369â†’                }
370â†’                // insert \n after multi-line value
371â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
372â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
373â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
374â†’                if (
375â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
376â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
377â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
378â†’                    // value(
379â†’                    // ), // a comment
380â†’                    // c, d
381â†’                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
382â†’                ) {
383â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
384â†’                }
385â†’            }
386â†’        }
387â†’    }
388â†’
389â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
390â†’        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
391â†’            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
392â†’
393â†’    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
394â†’        node: ASTNode,
395â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
396â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
397â†’    ) {
398â†’        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
399â†’            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
400â†’            node
401â†’                .children()
402â†’                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
403â†’                .forEach { typeProjection ->
404â†’                    typeProjection
405â†’                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
406â†’                        .let { prevSibling ->
407â†’                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
408â†’                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
409â†’                                if (autoCorrect) {
410â†’                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
411â†’                                }
412â†’                            }
413â†’                        }
414â†’                }
415â†’
416â†’            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
417â†’            node
418â†’                .findChildByType(GT)
419â†’                ?.let { closingAngle ->
420â†’                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
421â†’                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
422â†’                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
423â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
424â†’                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
425â†’                        }
426â†’                    }
427â†’                }
428â†’
429â†’            Unit
430â†’        }
431â†’    }
432â†’
433â†’    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
434â†’        node: ASTNode,
435â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437â†’    ) {
438â†’        node
439â†’            .treeParent
440â†’            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
441â†’            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
442â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
443â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
444â†’            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
445â†’            ?.let {
446â†’                // rewriting
447â†’                // """
448â†’                //     text
449â†’                // _""".trimIndent()
450â†’                // to
451â†’                // """
452â†’                //     text
453â†’                // _
454â†’                // """.trimIndent()
455â†’                emit(
456â†’                    node.startOffset,
457â†’                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
458â†’                    true,
459â†’                )
460â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
461â†’                    node as LeafPsiElement
462â†’                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
463â†’                }
464â†’                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
465â†’            }
466â†’    }
467â†’
468â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
471â†’        //     find matching rToken
472â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473â†’        // return false
474â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477â†’            // special cases:
478â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
479â†’            // })
480â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,
481â†’            // 2, 3)
482â†’            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
483â†’        }
484â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
485â†’            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
486â†’            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
487â†’            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
488â†’        }
489â†’        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
490â†’            return true
491â†’        }
492â†’        return false
493â†’    }
494â†’
495â†’    private fun rearrangeArrow(
496â†’        node: ASTNode,
497â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
498â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
499â†’    ) {
500â†’        val p = node.treeParent
501â†’        if (
502â†’            // check
503â†’            // `{ p -> ... }`
504â†’            // and
505â†’            // `when { m -> ... }`
506â†’            // only
507â†’            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
508â†’            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
509â†’            !p.textContains('\n') ||
510â†’            // permit
511â†’            // when {
512â†’            //     m -> 0 + d({
513â†’            //     })
514â†’            // }
515â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
516â†’            // permit
517â†’            // when (this) {
518â†’            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
519â†’            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
520â†’            //     -> true
521â†’            // }
522â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
523â†’        ) {
524â†’            return
525â†’        }
526â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
527â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
528â†’        }
529â†’        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
530â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
531â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.indent())
532â†’        }
533â†’    }
534â†’
535â†’    private fun insertNewLineAfterSemi(
536â†’        node: ASTNode,
537â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
538â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
539â†’    ) {
540â†’        val previousCodeLeaf = node.prevCodeLeaf()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
541â†’        val nextCodeLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ?: return
542â†’        if (previousCodeLeaf.treeParent.elementType == ENUM_ENTRY && nextCodeLeaf.elementType == RBRACE) {
543â†’            // Allow
544â†’            // enum class INDEX2 { ONE, TWO, THREE; }
545â†’            return
546â†’        }
547â†’        if (noNewLineInClosedRange(previousCodeLeaf, nextCodeLeaf)) {
548â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit, previousCodeLeaf.indent())
549â†’            node
550â†’                .treeParent
551â†’                .takeIf { it.elementType == BLOCK }
552â†’                ?.let { block ->
553â†’                    beforeVisitBlock(block, autoCorrect, emit)
554â†’                    block
555â†’                        .treeParent
556â†’                        .takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
557â†’                        ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
558â†’                        ?.let { arrow -> rearrangeArrow(arrow, autoCorrect, emit) }
559â†’                }
560â†’        }
561â†’    }
562â†’
563â†’    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
564â†’        node: ASTNode,
565â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
566â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
567â†’        indent: String,
568â†’    ) {
569â†’        emit(
570â†’            node.startOffset - 1,
571â†’            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
572â†’            true,
573â†’        )
574â†’        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
575â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
576â†’            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indent)
577â†’        }
578â†’    }
579â†’
580â†’    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
581â†’        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
582â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
583â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
584â†’        indent: String? = null,
585â†’        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
586â†’    ) {
587â†’        emit(
588â†’            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
589â†’            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
590â†’            true,
591â†’        )
592â†’        LOGGER.trace {
593â†’            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
594â†’        }
595â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
596â†’            val tempIndent = indent ?: (indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
597â†’            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(tempIndent)
598â†’        }
599â†’    }

601â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
602â†’        for (child in node.children()) {
603â†’            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
604â†’                val v = child.text
605â†’                if (v == "\n") {
606â†’                    return true
607â†’                }
608â†’            }
609â†’        }
610â†’        return false
611â†’    }

613â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
614â†’        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
615â†’        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
616â†’            textContains('\n')
617â†’        } else {
618â†’            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
619â†’                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
620â†’        }
621â†’    }
622â†’
623â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
624â†’
625â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
626â†’
627â†’    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
628â†’        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
629â†’            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
630â†’
631â†’    /**
632â†’     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
633â†’     *     for (foo in listOf(
634â†’     *         "foo-1",
635â†’     *         "foo-2"
636â†’     *     )) { ... }
637â†’     * but reject:
638â†’     *     for (
639â†’     *         foo in listOf(
640â†’     *             "foo-1",
641â†’     *             "foo-2"
642â†’     *         )
643â†’     *     ) { ... }
644â†’     */
645â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
646â†’        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
647â†’            return false
648â†’        }
649â†’        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
650â†’            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
651â†’        }
652â†’        require(elementType == LPAR) {
653â†’            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
654â†’        }
655â†’
656â†’        var node: ASTNode? = this
657â†’        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
658â†’            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
659â†’                return false
660â†’            }
661â†’            node = node.nextSibling()
662â†’        }
663â†’        return true
664â†’    }
665â†’
666â†’    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
667â†’        node: ASTNode,
668â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
669â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
670â†’    ) {
671â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
672â†’            val lbrace =
673â†’                node
674â†’                    .getStartOfBlock()
675â†’                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
676â†’                    ?: return
677â†’            node
678â†’                .getEndOfBlock()
679â†’                ?.takeUnless { it.isPrecededByNewline() }
680â†’                ?.let { rbrace ->
681â†’                    if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
682â†’                        requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
683â†’                            rbrace,
684â†’                            autoCorrect,
685â†’                            emit,
686â†’                            indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node),
687â†’                        )
688â†’                    }
689â†’                }
690â†’        }
691â†’    }
692â†’
693â†’    private fun ASTNode.followedByNewline() = nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
694â†’
695â†’    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByNewline() = prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
696â†’
697â†’    private fun ASTNode.getStartOfBlock() =
698â†’        firstChildLeafOrSelf()
699â†’            .let { node ->
700â†’                if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
701â†’                    // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
702â†’                    node
703â†’                } else {
704â†’                    // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
705â†’                    node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
706â†’                }
707â†’            }
708â†’
709â†’    private fun ASTNode.getEndOfBlock() =
710â†’        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
711â†’            .let { node ->
712â†’                if (node.elementType == RBRACE && treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
713â†’                    // WHEN-entry block have LBRACE and RBRACE as first and last elements
714â†’                    node
715â†’                } else {
716â†’                    // Other blocks have LBRACE and RBRACE as siblings of the block
717â†’                    node.nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
718â†’                }
719â†’            }
720â†’
721â†’    private companion object {
722â†’        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
723â†’        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
724â†’        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
725â†’            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
726â†’                RTOKEN_SET.types,
727â†’            ).toMap()

729â†’}

content.text:
Good, no lint errors. Now let me verify the fix is correct by examining a few scenarios:

1. **Single-line lambda with arrow**: `{ size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }` - The `FUNCTION_LITERAL` has no newlines between `{` and `->`, so `noNewlineBetweenLbraceAndArrow()` returns `true`, and `rearrangeArrow` returns early. âś“

2. **Multiline lambda with arrow where params are on separate lines**:
   ```
   {
       a,
       b,
       c ->
       doSomething()
   }
   ```
   The `FUNCTION_LITERAL` has newlines between `{` and `->`, so `noNewlineBetweenLbraceAndArrow()` returns `false`, and `rearrangeArrow` proceeds to enforce newlines. âś“

3. **Lambda where body is multiline but params are on same line as `{`**:
   ```
   { size -> 
       icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await()
   }
   ```
   The `FUNCTION_LITERAL` has no newlines between `{` and `->` (they're on the same line), so `noNewlineBetweenLbraceAndArrow()` returns `true`, and `rearrangeArrow` returns early. The newline after `->` is already there, so this format is kept. âś“

Now, wait - I need to reconsider scenario 3. If the lambda body has been wrapped such that there IS a newline after `->`, but the params are on the same line as `{`, should `rearrangeArrow` still handle the `}` placement? Let me look at that case more carefully.

In scenario 3, `rearrangeArrow` returns early. But the `afterVisitChildNodes` for the BLOCK would handle the `}` placement through `getEndOfBlock`. Let me check...

Actually looking at the test on line 2550-2572:
```kotlin
val fooBar =
    fooBar()
        .map { foo, bar -> print(foo); print(bar) }
```
This gets reformatted to:
```kotlin
val fooBar =
    fooBar()
        .map { foo, bar ->
            print(foo)
            print(bar)
        }
```

This case is handled by `insertNewLineAfterSemi` which processes the `;`. It inserts a newline after `;`, making the block multiline. Then it calls `rearrangeArrow`. With my fix, since there's no newline between `{` and `->` (they're on the same line), `rearrangeArrow` would now return early and NOT insert a newline after `->`.

Wait, that would break this test case! The expected behavior is that `{ foo, bar -> print(foo); print(bar) }` should be reformatted with a newline after `->`.

Hmm, let me reconsider. The issue is that after `insertNewLineAfterSemi` inserts a newline after `;`, the code becomes:
```
{ foo, bar -> print(foo)
    print(bar) }
```

At this point, `insertNewLineAfterSemi` calls `rearrangeArrow`. The arrow's parent (FUNCTION_LITERAL) now contains a newline (in the body). But `noNewlineBetweenLbraceAndArrow()` checks between LBRACE and ARROW - there's no newline there. So with my fix, `rearrangeArrow` returns early.

But wait, we still need the newline after `->` in this case! The current expected behavior is that `{ foo, bar -> print(foo); print(bar) }` gets reformatted to:
```kotlin
{ foo, bar ->
    print(foo)
    print(bar)
}
```

So my fix would break this case. I need a more nuanced approach.

Let me think about what the actual difference is between the scenarios:

1. `{ size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }` - Should NOT be changed. The body is on the same line as `->`.
2. `{ foo, bar -> print(foo); print(bar) }` - The semicolon causes it to be wrapped. After the `;` is replaced with newline, we need newline after `->` too.

The key difference is: In case 1, the body after `->` is still on a single line (same line as `->`). In case 2, the body after `->` has been wrapped to multiple lines.

Wait, but both have `p.textContains('\n')` being true only in case 2 (after the semicolon handling). In case 1, the original code has no newlines at all, so `p.textContains('\n')` is false and `rearrangeArrow` already returns early. My fix shouldn't affect case 1 at all!

Let me re-think the original issue. The user's code is `{ size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }`. This is fully single-line. `p.textContains('\n')` is false. `rearrangeArrow` already returns early in the ORIGINAL code. So what's causing the error?

Let me re-read the issue description more carefully:

> With version 0.50.0, the below code now produces an error because of the lack of newline after the {:

So the error is about the `{`, not the `->`. The error message is something like `Missing newline after "{"`.

Looking at `rearrangeBlock` (which handles LPAR and LBRACKET, but NOT LBRACE for lambda), and `beforeVisitBlock` (which handles BLOCK nodes)... Wait, `rearrangeBlock` IS called for LBRACE too! Let me check again:

No, looking at line 136: `LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)`. It's NOT called for LBRACE.

But LBRACE IS in `rearrangeBlock`'s handling:
```kotlin
// keep { p ->
// }
(node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
```

Wait, that's inside `rearrangeBlock`, but `rearrangeBlock` is only called for LPAR and LBRACKET, not LBRACE. This condition can never be true based on the `when` statement. Unless there's another path...

Actually wait, let me look more carefully at the `rearrangeBlock` function. Line 206: `val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]`. If `node.elementType` is LPAR, then `rElementType` is RPAR. If LBRACKET, then RBRACKET. LBRACE is NOT handled by this function since it's only called for LPAR and LBRACKET.

But wait, there IS a condition checking `node.elementType == LBRACE` on line 227. This would never be true... unless... Actually, maybe I'm wrong. Let me look at the `when` statement again:

```kotlin
when (node.elementType) {
    BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
    LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
    ...
}
```

So `rearrangeBlock` is only called for LPAR and LBRACKET. The LBRACE check inside is dead code. Unless at some point it was called for LBRACE too.

OK, let me think about this differently. Maybe the issue is about the ParameterListWrappingRule, not the WrappingRule.

Let me look at the ParameterListWrappingRule again. For `ktlint_official` code style, when a VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST in a FUNCTION_LITERAL is on a line exceeding max line length, the rule wraps each parameter to a new line:

```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.needToWrapParameterList() =
    when {
        hasNoParameters() -> false
        codeStyle != ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle() -> false
        codeStyle == ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression() ->
            false
        textContains('\n') -> true
        codeStyle == ktlint_official && containsAnnotatedParameter() -> true
        isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() -> true
        else -> false
    }
```

For `ktlint_official`, if `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression()` returns false and `isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength()` returns true, the parameter list gets wrapped. This inserts newlines before each parameter, making the lambda multiline.

Let me look at `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression()`:

```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression(): Boolean {
    require(elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
    return firstChildLeafOrSelf()
        .let { startOfFunctionLiteral ->
            treeParent
                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
                ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
                ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
                ?.leaves()
                ?.takeWhile { it != startOfFunctionLiteral }
                ?.none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
                ?: false
        }
}
```

This returns true only when the function literal is inside a VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST of a CALL_EXPRESSION and starts on the same line as the closing parenthesis. But what about standalone lambdas or lambdas assigned to variables? For example:

```kotlin
val foo = { size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }
```

Here, the FUNCTION_LITERAL is inside a LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, which is the value of a property. The `prevCodeLeaf()` of the FUNCTION_LITERAL would be `=` or something, not a closing paren of a call expression. So `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression()` returns false.

Then, if the line exceeds max line length, `isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength()` returns true, and `needToWrapParameterList()` returns true. The parameter list gets wrapped, making the function literal multiline. Then `rearrangeArrow` in the wrapping rule kicks in.

So the fix needs to address BOTH:
1. The ParameterListWrappingRule wrapping params in function literals unnecessarily
2. The WrappingRule's `rearrangeArrow` forcing newlines around `->` when it shouldn't

Actually, thinking about it more carefully, there might be other scenarios where the body of a lambda becomes multiline (not due to wrapping of parameters) and `rearrangeArrow` should kick in. For example, the semicolon case we discussed earlier.

Let me reconsider my fix. In the semicolon case:
```
{ foo, bar -> print(foo); print(bar) }
```
After `insertNewLineAfterSemi` runs:
1. A newline is inserted after `;`
2. `beforeVisitBlock` is called (returns early for function literal)
3. `rearrangeArrow` is called

At step 3, with my fix, since there's no newline between `{` and `->`, `rearrangeArrow` returns early. This means the arrow won't get a newline after it.

But the expected behavior IS to add a newline after `->` in this case. So my fix breaks this.

Hmm, let me look at the actual expected behavior more carefully. Let me re-read the test:

```kotlin
val fooBar =
    fooBar()
        .map { foo, bar -> print(foo); print(bar) }
```
Expected formatted code:
```kotlin
val fooBar =
    fooBar()
        .map { foo, bar ->
            print(foo)
            print(bar)
        }
```

Wait, in this expected format, `{ foo, bar ->` is still on the same line as `{`. The `->` is followed by a newline. And the `}` is on its own line. So for this case, `rearrangeArrow` SHOULD add a newline after `->`.

But for the issue's case `{ size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }`, `rearrangeArrow` should NOT add a newline after `->`.

The difference is:
- In the semicolon case, the body (after `->`) contains a newline (because the `;` was replaced)
- In the issue case, the body (after `->`) does NOT contain a newline

So perhaps instead of checking for newlines between `{` and `->`, I should check for newlines after `->` (in the body). But that's essentially what `p.textContains('\n')` already does - it returns true if ANY part of the function literal contains a newline.

Wait, actually the key insight is: the issue's code `{ size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }` has NO newlines anywhere. So `p.textContains('\n')` is false, and `rearrangeArrow` already returns early. My fix is actually NOT needed for the original issue code.

But the issue says this code produces an error with v0.50.0. Let me re-examine WHY the error occurs. Maybe the error is not from the wrapping rule but from another rule.

Let me re-read the issue statement:
> With version 0.50.0, the below code now produces an error because of the lack of newline after the {:

The "lack of newline after the {" - this could be from any rule, not just the wrapping rule. Could it be from the `ParameterListWrappingRule`?

Actually, the ParameterListWrappingRule wraps PARAMETERS - it inserts newlines before `VALUE_PARAMETER` nodes, not after `{`. The error message from ParameterListWrappingRule is "Parameter should start on a newline" or "Unnecessary newline before \"(\"" or "Missing newline before \")\"".

Maybe the error is from yet another rule. Let me check what rules would flag this code...

Actually, let me look at this from the `isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength` perspective. The function checks if the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST's line exceeds max line length. If it does, `wrapParameterList` is called:

```kotlin
private fun wrapParameterList(node: ASTNode, ...) {
    node.children().forEach { child -> wrapParameterInList(child, emit, autoCorrect) }
}
```

And `wrapParameterInList` processes each child, including VALUE_PARAMETER nodes. For a VALUE_PARAMETER node, it checks if the previous leaf is whitespace. If the whitespace doesn't contain a newline, it inserts one. This would change:

```
{ size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }
```
to:
```
{
    size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }
```

Wait, that inserts a newline before `size`, but the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST in a function literal doesn't have LPAR/RPAR. So only the VALUE_PARAMETER case applies. Let me trace through:

For `wrapParameterInList(child)` where `child` is VALUE_PARAMETER "size":
- `child.elementType` is VALUE_PARAMETER
- `intendedIndent(child)` calculates indent
- `prevLeaf` is the whitespace " " between `{` and `size`
- `prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace` -> true
- `prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")` -> false (it's just " ")
- So it emits "Parameter should start on a newline" and inserts a newline before `size`

So ParameterListWrappingRule adds a newline before `size`, making the code:
```
{
    size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }
```

But wait, there's no RPAR in a function literal's VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, so the RPAR case doesn't apply. But the function literal now has a newline between `{` and `->`.

Then, in the same pass, when the WrappingRule processes the ARROW node, `rearrangeArrow` is called. Now `p.textContains('\n')` IS true (because of the newline inserted by ParameterListWrappingRule). And my new check `p.noNewlineBetweenLbraceAndArrow()` would return... well, there IS now a newline between LBRACE and ARROW (inserted by ParameterListWrappingRule). So `noNewlineBetweenLbraceAndArrow()` returns false, and `rearrangeArrow` proceeds.

Hmm, but the issue says the wrapping should NOT happen. The maintainer says: "With fix of #2106 you original code sample will be kept unchanged."

So the fix should be in the `ParameterListWrappingRule`, not in the `WrappingRule`. The ParameterListWrappingRule should not wrap parameters in a function literal when the parameter list is on a single line. Let me look at the conditions again:

```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.needToWrapParameterList() =
    when {
        hasNoParameters() -> false
        codeStyle != ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle() -> false
        codeStyle == ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression() ->
            false
        textContains('\n') -> true
        codeStyle == ktlint_official && containsAnnotatedParameter() -> true
        isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() -> true
        else -> false
    }
```

For `ktlint_official`, the only exemption for function literals is `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression()`. This is a very specific check. The fix should be broader: for ALL function literals in `ktlint_official`, if the parameter list is on a single line (not already multiline), don't wrap it just because the line exceeds max length.

Wait, but should we really prevent wrapping for ALL function literals? The maintainer says the wrapping should only be applied "when having a function literal with parameters on separate lines." This suggests:

- If the function literal's parameters are already on separate lines, wrapping is OK
- If the parameters are on a single line, don't wrap them

The condition `textContains('\n') -> true` handles the first case. But `isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() -> true` also triggers wrapping for single-line parameters in function literals.

The fix should be: For `ktlint_official` code style, when the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST is part of a FUNCTION_LITERAL and is currently on a single line, don't wrap it based on max line length.

Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe the fix should be simpler: Just extend the `isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle()` check to apply for ALL code styles, not just non-ktlint_official. Or add a similar check for `ktlint_official`.

Let me look at what `isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle` does:

```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle(): Boolean {
    require(elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
    return treeParent?.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL
}
```

It simply checks if the parameter list is inside a function literal. For non-ktlint_official, ALL function literals are exempted. For ktlint_official, only the specific case of `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression` is exempted.

But we want ktlint_official to also handle function literals that have single-line parameter lists. The fix in ParameterListWrappingRule should prevent wrapping of single-line function literal parameter lists when it's purely because of max line length.

Let me revert my wrapping rule change first (it's wrong) and instead fix the ParameterListWrappingRule.

Actually wait, let me think about whether the WrappingRule fix is also needed. Even if the ParameterListWrappingRule doesn't wrap the parameters, there might be other ways the function literal becomes multiline where the parameters are still on the same line as `{`. My fix in the WrappingRule would be a safety net.

But actually, the test case with semicolons showed that the WrappingRule fix would break existing behavior. So let me revert the WrappingRule fix.

Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the semicolon test case:

```kotlin
.map { foo, bar -> print(foo); print(bar) }
```

After `insertNewLineAfterSemi` processes the `;`:
1. Newline is inserted after `;` (in the BLOCK)
2. `beforeVisitBlock` is called - returns early for function literal
3. `rearrangeArrow` is called

At step 3, `p` (FUNCTION_LITERAL) now contains a newline (in the body). And `noNewlineBetweenLbraceAndArrow()` would check: is there a newline between LBRACE and ARROW? The LBRACE is `{`, then whitespace ` `, then VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, then whitespace ` `, then ARROW `->`. None of these whitespaces contain newlines. So `noNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, arrow)` returns true, meaning `noNewlineBetweenLbraceAndArrow()` returns true, and `rearrangeArrow` returns early.

But we WANT `rearrangeArrow` to insert a newline after `->` in this case! The expected output has a newline after `->`.

So my fix in the WrappingRule IS too broad. It prevents `rearrangeArrow` from adding newlines in cases where they should be added.

OK, so let me take a different approach. The fix should be in the `ParameterListWrappingRule`, to prevent it from wrapping single-line parameter lists in function literals.

Let me revert the WrappingRule change and fix the ParameterListWrappingRule instead:

 90â†’/**
 91â†’ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 92â†’ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 93â†’ *
 94â†’ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 95â†’ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 96â†’ * indentation rule is not run.
 97â†’ */
 98â†’public class WrappingRule :
 99â†’    StandardRule(
100â†’        id = "wrapping",
101â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
102â†’            setOf(
103â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
104â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
105â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
106â†’            ),
107â†’        visitorModifiers =
108â†’            setOf(
109â†’                RunAfterRule(
110â†’                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
111â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
112â†’                ),
113â†’            ),
114â†’    ) {
115â†’    private var line = 1
116â†’    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
117â†’    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
118â†’
119â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
120â†’        line = 1
121â†’        indentConfig =
122â†’            IndentConfig(
123â†’                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
124â†’                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
125â†’            )
126â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
127â†’    }
128â†’
129â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
130â†’        node: ASTNode,
131â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
132â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
133â†’    ) {
134â†’        when (node.elementType) {
135â†’            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136â†’            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137â†’            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
138â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139â†’            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
140â†’            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
141â†’            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
142â†’            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
143â†’            SEMICOLON -> insertNewLineAfterSemi(node, autoCorrect, emit)
144â†’        }
145â†’    }
146â†’
147â†’    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
148â†’        node: ASTNode,
149â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
151â†’    ) {
152â†’        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
153â†’
154â†’        val lbrace =
155â†’            node
156â†’                .getStartOfBlock()
157â†’                ?.takeIf { it.elementType == LBRACE }
158â†’                ?: return
159â†’        if (lbrace.followedByNewline() ||
160â†’            lbrace.followedByEolComment() ||
161â†’            lbrace.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY)
162â†’        ) {
163â†’            // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
164â†’            return
165â†’        }
166â†’
167â†’        node
168â†’            .takeUnless { it.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
169â†’            ?.getEndOfBlock()
170â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == RBRACE }
171â†’            ?.let { rbrace ->
172â†’                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(lbrace, rbrace)) {
173â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
174â†’                }
175â†’            }
176â†’
177â†’        if (maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
178â†’            val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
179â†’                node
180â†’                    .leaves(false)
181â†’                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
182â†’                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
183â†’            val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
184â†’                node
185â†’                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
186â†’                    .leavesIncludingSelf()
187â†’                    .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
188â†’                    .sumOf { it.textLength }
189â†’            if (lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength) {
190â†’                requireNewlineAfterLeaf(lbrace, autoCorrect, emit)
191â†’            }
192â†’        }
193â†’    }
194â†’
195â†’    private fun ASTNode.followedByEolComment() =
196â†’        null !=
197â†’            leaves()
198â†’                .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
199â†’                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
200â†’
201â†’    private fun rearrangeBlock(
202â†’        node: ASTNode,
203â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
204â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
205â†’    ) {
206â†’        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
207â†’        var newlineInBetween = false
208â†’        var parameterListInBetween = false
209â†’        var numberOfArgs = 0
210â†’        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
211â†’        // matching ), ] or }
212â†’        val r = node.nextSibling {
213â†’            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
214â†’            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
215â†’            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
216â†’            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
217â†’            if (isValueArgument) {
218â†’                numberOfArgs++
219â†’                firstArg = it
220â†’            }
221â†’            it.elementType == rElementType
222â†’        }!!
223â†’        if (
224â†’            !newlineInBetween ||
225â†’            // keep { p ->
226â†’            // }
227â†’            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
228â†’            // keep ({
229â†’            // }) and (object : C {
230â†’            // })
231â†’            (
232â†’                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
233â†’                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
234â†’                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
235â†’                )
236â†’        ) {
237â†’            return
238â†’        }
239â†’        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
240â†’            // keep:
241â†’            // for (foo in listOf(
242â†’            //     "foo-1",
243â†’            //     "foo-2"
244â†’            // )) { ... }
245â†’            // but reject:
246â†’            // for (
247â†’            //     foo in listOf(
248â†’            //         "foo-1",
249â†’            //         "foo-2"
250â†’            //     )
251â†’            // ) { ... }
252â†’            return
253â†’        }
254â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
255â†’                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
256â†’                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
257â†’                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
258â†’                    it.textLength > 0
259â†’            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
260â†’            // IDEA quirk:
261â†’            // if (true &&
262â†’            //     true
263â†’            // ) {
264â†’            // }
265â†’            // instead of
266â†’            // if (
267â†’            //     true &&
268â†’            //     true
269â†’            // ) {
270â†’            // }
271â†’            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
272â†’        ) {
273â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
274â†’        }
275â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
276â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, indentConfig.parentIndentOf(node))
277â†’        }
278â†’    }
279â†’
280â†’    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
281â†’        node: ASTNode,
282â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
283â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
284â†’    ) {
285â†’        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
286â†’        if (
287â†’            node.textContains('\n') &&
288â†’            entries.size > 1 &&
289â†’            // e.g.
290â†’            //
291â†’            // class A : B, C,
292â†’            //     D
293â†’            // or
294â†’            // class A : B, C({
295â†’            // }), D
296â†’            //
297â†’            // but not
298â†’            //
299â†’            // class A : B, C, D({
300â†’            // })
301â†’            !(
302â†’                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
303â†’                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
304â†’                )
305â†’        ) {
306â†’            // put space after :
307â†’            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
308â†’                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
309â†’                if (
310â†’                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
311â†’                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
312â†’                ) {
313â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
314â†’                }
315â†’            }
316â†’            // put entries on separate lines
317â†’            for (c in node.children()) {
318â†’                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
319â†’                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
320â†’                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
321â†’                ) {
322â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
323â†’                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
324â†’                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
325â†’                        emit = emit,
326â†’                        indent = node.indent(),
327â†’                    )
328â†’                }
329â†’            }
330â†’        }
331â†’    }
332â†’
333â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
334â†’        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
335â†’            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
336â†’
337â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(
338â†’        node: ASTNode,
339â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
340â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
341â†’    ) {
342â†’        for (c in node.children()) {
343â†’            val hasLineBreak =
344â†’                when (c.elementType) {
345â†’                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
346â†’                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
347â†’                    else -> false
348â†’                }
349â†’            if (hasLineBreak) {
350â†’                // rearrange
351â†’                //
352â†’                // a, b, value(
353â†’                // ), c, d
354â†’                //
355â†’                // to
356â†’                //
357â†’                // a, b,
358â†’                // value(
359â†’                // ),
360â†’                // c, d
361â†’
362â†’                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
363â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
364â†’                if (
365â†’                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
366â†’                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
367â†’                ) {
368â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
369â†’                }
370â†’                // insert \n after multi-line value
371â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
372â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
373â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
374â†’                if (
375â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
376â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
377â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
378â†’                    // value(
379â†’                    // ), // a comment
380â†’                    // c, d
381â†’                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
382â†’                ) {
383â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
384â†’                }
385â†’            }
386â†’        }
387â†’    }
388â†’
389â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
390â†’        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
391â†’            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
392â†’
393â†’    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
394â†’        node: ASTNode,
395â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
396â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
397â†’    ) {
398â†’        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
399â†’            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
400â†’            node
401â†’                .children()
402â†’                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
403â†’                .forEach { typeProjection ->
404â†’                    typeProjection
405â†’                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
406â†’                        .let { prevSibling ->
407â†’                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
408â†’                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
409â†’                                if (autoCorrect) {
410â†’                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
411â†’                                }
412â†’                            }
413â†’                        }
414â†’                }
415â†’
416â†’            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
417â†’            node
418â†’                .findChildByType(GT)
419â†’                ?.let { closingAngle ->
420â†’                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
421â†’                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
422â†’                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
423â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
424â†’                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.siblingIndentOf(node))
425â†’                        }
426â†’                    }
427â†’                }
428â†’
429â†’            Unit
430â†’        }
431â†’    }
432â†’
433â†’    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
434â†’        node: ASTNode,
435â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437â†’    ) {
438â†’        node
439â†’            .treeParent
440â†’            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
441â†’            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
442â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
443â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
444â†’            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
445â†’            ?.let {
446â†’                // rewriting
447â†’                // """
448â†’                //     text
449â†’                // _""".trimIndent()
450â†’                // to
451â†’                // """
452â†’                //     text
453â†’                // _
454â†’                // """.trimIndent()
455â†’                emit(
456â†’                    node.startOffset,
457â†’                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
458â†’                    true,
459â†’                )
460â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
461â†’                    node as LeafPsiElement
462â†’                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
463â†’                }
464â†’                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
465â†’            }
466â†’    }
467â†’
468â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
471â†’        //     find matching rToken
472â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473â†’        // return false
474â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477â†’            // special cases:
478â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
479â†’            // })
480â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,
481â†’            // 2, 3)
482â†’            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
483â†’        }
484â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
485â†’            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
486â†’            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
487â†’            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
488â†’        }
489â†’        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
490â†’            return true
491â†’        }
492â†’        return false
493â†’    }
494â†’
495â†’    private fun rearrangeArrow(
496â†’        node: ASTNode,
497â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
498â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
499â†’    ) {
500â†’        val p = node.treeParent
501â†’        if (
502â†’            // check
503â†’            // `{ p -> ... }`
504â†’            // and
505â†’            // `when { m -> ... }`
506â†’            // only
507â†’            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
508â†’            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
509â†’            !p.textContains('\n') ||
510â†’            // permit
511â†’            // when {
512â†’            //     m -> 0 + d({
513â†’            //     })
514â†’            // }
515â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
516â†’            // permit
517â†’            // when (this) {
518â†’            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
519â†’            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
520â†’            //     -> true
521â†’            // }
522â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true) ||
523â†’            // permit
524â†’            // { size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }
525â†’            // Only force the multiline arrow format when the value parameter list of the function literal is
526â†’            // multiline. If the value parameter list is on a single line (no newline between the LBRACE and
527â†’            // the ARROW), then do not force the multiline arrow format.
528â†’            (p.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL && p.noNewlineBetweenLbraceAndArrow())
529â†’        ) {
530â†’            return
531â†’        }
532â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
533â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
534â†’        }
535â†’        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
536â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {

content.text:
OK, the file currently still has my changes. Let me think about this more carefully before deciding what to do.

Let me reconsider the semicolon test case. In `insertNewLineAfterSemi`, after inserting the newline after `;`, the code calls `rearrangeArrow`. With my fix, `rearrangeArrow` returns early because there's no newline between `{` and `->`. But the expected output DOES have a newline after `->`.

So who adds the newline after `->` in the expected output? Let me re-examine: After `insertNewLineAfterSemi`:
1. The semicolon is replaced with a newline in the block
2. `beforeVisitBlock` is called - for function literals it returns early
3. `rearrangeArrow` is called - with my fix, it returns early

So without `rearrangeArrow`, the code becomes:
```
{ foo, bar -> print(foo)
    print(bar) }
```

Then `afterVisitChildNodes` for BLOCK would check if the RBRACE needs a newline. But `getStartOfBlock()` returns ARROW (not LBRACE), so it returns early.

So the `}` wouldn't get a newline either. The formatted code would be wrong.

Hmm, but this test already passes with the current code (without my fix). The `insertNewLineAfterSemi` calls `rearrangeArrow` which then adds the newlines after `->` and before `}`. My fix would break this test.

OK so I need to be more careful. Let me think about what EXACTLY the right fix is.

The actual scenario described in the issue is: ParameterListWrappingRule wraps the single parameter `size` in a function literal, making the lambda multiline. Then WrappingRule's `rearrangeArrow` forces newlines around `->`.

The proper fix should prevent ParameterListWrappingRule from wrapping single parameters in function literals when the parameter list is on a single line. This way, the function literal stays single-line, and `rearrangeArrow` returns early (because `p.textContains('\n')` is false).

Let me also consider: is there any OTHER reason the function literal could become multiline? The `beforeVisitBlock` returns early for function literals. The `rearrangeBlock` is only called for LPAR/LBRACKET. The `rearrangeArrow` only acts when the function literal already has newlines. So the main source of newlines being added to a function literal is:
1. ParameterListWrappingRule wrapping parameters
2. Other rules that run before WrappingRule
3. The `insertNewLineAfterSemi` in WrappingRule (semicolons)

For case 3 (semicolons), my fix would break the behavior. For case 1 (ParameterListWrappingRule), fixing the rule itself prevents the issue at the source.

So the fix should be in `ParameterListWrappingRule`. Let me revert the WrappingRule to its original state and fix the ParameterListWrappingRule instead.

Let me write the original WrappingRule back:

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. For `ktlint_official`:
- `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression()` only returns `false` (i.e., allows skipping) when the function literal starts on the same line as the closing paren of a preceding call expression. But this doesn't cover all cases of function literals.
- `isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength()` can trigger wrapping for ANY function literal's parameter list, including standalone lambdas or lambdas not preceded by call expressions.

The fix: We need to also skip wrapping for function literals where the parameter list is currently on a single line (not already multiline). For `ktlint_official`, the condition should be expanded.

Looking at the issue discussion, the maintainer explicitly states:
> The format above should only be applied when having a function literal with parameters on separate lines.

And:
> With fix of #2106 you original code sample will be kept unchanged.

So for function literals, the parameter list should only be wrapped if it already contains newlines (i.e., `textContains('\n')` is true). It should NOT be wrapped just because the line exceeds max line length.

The simplest fix is to change `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression` to simply check if the parameter list is part of a function literal (for ktlint_official). Or, we can add a new condition that handles the general case.

Let me modify the `needToWrapParameterList` method to skip wrapping for function literals in ktlint_official when the parameter list doesn't already contain newlines:

content.text:
The fix I want to make: Change the condition so that for `ktlint_official`, ALL function literals with single-line parameter lists skip wrapping, not just those starting on the same line as a closing paren.

Actually, wait. I need to look at the condition more carefully. Let me understand the different cases:

For `ktlint_official`:
1. `hasNoParameters() -> false` - Skip if no params
2. `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression() -> false` - Skip if function literal is preceded by a call expression's closing paren on same line
3. `textContains('\n') -> true` - Wrap if already multiline
4. `containsAnnotatedParameter() -> true` - Wrap if annotated params
5. `isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() -> true` - Wrap if line too long
6. `false` - Don't wrap otherwise

The problem is step 5: it wraps function literal parameter lists when the line is too long, but wrapping the parameter list of a function literal doesn't help reduce line length (the parameter is already on the same line). Instead, it creates the weird formatting described in the issue.

The fix should add a condition before step 5 that skips function literals in ktlint_official when the parameter list doesn't already contain newlines. Or, equivalently, change step 2 to cover ALL function literals, not just those preceded by call expressions.

Actually, looking more carefully, condition 2 is specifically for the case where the function literal IS preceded by a call expression. For standalone lambdas or lambdas used in assignments, this check fails. So the fix should extend condition 2 to cover all function literals.

Wait, but maybe there's a reason for the specific check. Let me look at it again:

The `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression` function checks if the lambda is passed as a trailing lambda to a function call and starts on the same line. For example:
```kotlin
someFunction() { size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }
```
In this case, the function doesn't want to wrap the parameter because it's a trailing lambda.

But for:
```kotlin
val foo = { size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }
```
This check returns false because there's no preceding call expression.

The fix should be: For `ktlint_official`, skip wrapping for ALL function literals whose parameter list doesn't already contain newlines, regardless of whether they're trailing lambdas or standalone.

Let me implement this:

        when {
            hasNoParameters() -> false
            codeStyle != ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle() -> false
            codeStyle == ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression() ->
                false
            textContains('\n') -> true
            codeStyle == ktlint_official && containsAnnotatedParameter() -> true
            isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() -> true
            else -> false
        }", "new_string": "    private fun ASTNode.needToWrapParameterList() =
        when {
            hasNoParameters() -> false
            codeStyle != ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle() -> false
            codeStyle == ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle() && !textContains('\n') -> false
            textContains('\n') -> true
            codeStyle == ktlint_official && containsAnnotatedParameter() -> true
            isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() -> true
            else -> false
        }"}

content.text:
The function `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression` is now unused because I replaced it. Actually, the new function `isPartOfFunctionLiteralWithSingleLineParameterList` covers its use case (and more broadly). The old function's behavior was a subset: it only returned true for function literals preceded by a call expression on the same line. The new function returns true for ALL function literals with single-line parameter lists.

However, the old function `isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression` is no longer used, which is fine - it was only used in the `needToWrapParameterList` condition that I replaced. But the linter warning tells me it's unused. Let me remove it.
